ranked_movies.title: The Thing
ranked_movies.reason: Extensively praised for its practical effects, body horror, and status as a sci-fi/horror classic. Frequently mentioned in 'favorite horror movies' lists and discussed for its iconic scenes.

ranked_movies.title: Hereditary
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently cited for its Oscar-worthy performances (Toni Collette, Alex Wolff) and its powerful, disturbing impact on audiences. A modern horror benchmark.

ranked_movies.title: Alien
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently listed as a top horror and sci-fi film, praised for its monster design and atmospheric tension. A cornerstone of the genre.

ranked_movies.title: The Shining
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned for its iconic performances (Shelley Duvall) and its lasting legacy as a psychological horror masterpiece.

ranked_movies.title: The Exorcist
ranked_movies.reason: A perennial favorite on 'best of' lists and a film often discussed for its cultural impact and terrifying scenes.

ranked_movies.title: Scream
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a favorite slasher franchise and a smart, meta-horror film that fans grew up with and adore.

ranked_movies.title: Halloween
ranked_movies.reason: Named as the classic slasher film and a foundational entry in the genre by multiple users.

ranked_movies.title: Evil Dead
ranked_movies.reason: The 2013 remake was specifically highlighted for its intense theater experience and gruesome impact, while the franchise as a whole is a fan favorite.

ranked_movies.title: The Ring
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed both for its effective scares and its villain's backstory. Noted for having a memorable theater experience.

ranked_movies.title: Terrifier
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned in the context of inappropriate child viewings, highlighting its extreme gore and notoriety.

ranked_movies.title: Midsommar
ranked_movies.reason: Praised for its cinematography, unique setting, and psychological depth. Also noted for its impactful theater experience.

ranked_movies.title: Carrie
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as a film where the villain has a valid grievance, making its story more tragic and impactful.

ranked_movies.title: Jaws
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned as a film that changed behavior (fear of water) and for Robert Shaw's Oscar-worthy performance.

ranked_movies.title: The Conjuring
ranked_movies.reason: Appears on multiple favorite lists and was the central movie in a discussion about poor theater etiquette with children.

ranked_movies.title: Get Out
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a must-see horror film and noted for Daniel Kaluuya's fantastic performance.

ranked_movies.title: The Autopsy of Jane Doe
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently recommended as an underrated gem and a very effective horror movie.

ranked_movies.title: REC
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended, especially for found-footage fans, and praised for its intense, well-executed scenes.

ranked_movies.title: Candyman
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a classic horror film and another example of a villain with a valid, sympathetic grievance.

ranked_movies.title: The Substance
ranked_movies.reason: A recent film praised for its wild scenes, body horror, and strong performances (Demi Moore).

ranked_movies.title: Event Horizon
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a cult classic and noted for its disturbing recovered footage scene and Warhammer 40k connections.
